title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards by outlining their approach to the issues raised. The response aims to address the recommendations made by the committee to ensure that AI aligns with public standards and expectations.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document examines the impact of using copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It aims to assess the effects of copyright infringement and potential licensing issues in AI development.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a policy on generative artificial intelligence in education, although the specifics are not provided. This policy is likely intended to outline the DfE's stance on the use of AI tools in educational settings, balancing benefits and potential risks.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This policy document outlines the government's plan to use artificial intelligence in the transport sector, focusing on both the benefits and potential risks. The plan is designed to guide collaboration between the Department for Transport and the transport industry to maximize AI's positive impact while mitigating its negative effects.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This policy report discusses the use of copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It is an update under the Data (Use and Access) Act, but further information is needed for a more detailed summary.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: This document outlines the government's plan to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in medical devices, following recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council. The government aims to ensure that AI-powered medical devices are safe and effective for use in the healthcare system.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications, such as exams and assessments. It is in response to a request from the government to provide guidance on this issue.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: This policy document outlines the government's response to a report from the House of Lords on artificial intelligence in the UK, where they discuss the current state and future plans for AI development. The government addresses the findings and recommendations of the report in their response to implement policies and regulations for the responsible development and use of AI in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The document outlines the framework and guidelines for a committee reviewing the intersection of artificial intelligence and public standards. It will inform the committee's examination of this topic through terms of reference and frequently asked questions.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has submitted its views on regulating AI for a government inquiry. The submission likely addresses issues around overseeing the development and deployment of artificial intelligence in various sectors.
